course_objectives: Applied Physics aims to give an understanding of this world both by observation and by prediction of the way in which objects behave. Concrete use of physical principles and analysis in various fields of engineering and technology are given prominence in the course content. The course will help the diploma engineers to apply the basic concepts and principles to solve broad-based engineering problems and to understand different technology based applications.

modules.title: Wave motion and its applications
topics: Wave motion: transverse and longitudinal waves with examples
Definitions: wave velocity, frequency, wavelength and their relationship
Sound and light waves and their properties
Wave equation: y = r sin ωt; amplitude, phase, phase difference
Principle of superposition and beats
Simple Harmonic Motion (SHM): displacement, velocity, acceleration, time period, frequency
Free, forced and resonant vibrations with examples
Acoustics of buildings: reverberation, reverberation time, echo, noise, coefficient of absorption, control methods and applications
Ultrasonic waves: introduction, properties, engineering and medical applications

modules.title: Optics
topics: Basic optical laws: reflection and refraction; refractive index
Image formation by mirrors and lenses; thin lenses; lens formula, power, magnification
Total internal reflection: critical angle, conditions, applications in optical fiber
Optical instruments: simple and compound microscope, astronomical telescope in normal adjustment; magnifying power

modules.title: Electrostatics
topics: Coulomb’s law; unit of charge
Electric field; electric lines of force and properties
Electric flux; electric potential and potential difference; Gauss’s law
Capacitors: working, capacitance and units; parallel plate capacitance
Series and parallel combination of capacitors (numericals)
Dielectrics and effect on capacitance; dielectric breakdown

modules.title: Current Electricity
topics: Electric current and units; DC and AC
Resistance and units; specific resistance, conductance, specific conductance
Series and parallel combinations of resistances
Factors affecting resistance of a wire; carbon resistors and color coding
Ohm’s law and verification; Kirchhoff’s laws
Terminal potential difference and EMF
Heating effect of current; electric power and energy with units (numericals)
Advantages of electric energy over other forms

modules.title: Electromagnetism
topics: Magnetic materials: diamagnetic, paramagnetic, ferromagnetic properties
Magnetic field and units; magnetic intensity; lines of force; magnetic flux and units; magnetization
Lorentz force on moving charge; force on current-carrying conductor
Moving coil galvanometer: principle, construction, working
Conversion of galvanometer into ammeter and voltmeter

modules.title: Semiconductor Physics
topics: Energy bands in solids
Types of materials: insulator, semiconductor, conductor
Intrinsic and extrinsic semiconductors
p-n junction and diode; V-I characteristics
Diode as rectifier: half-wave and full-wave (center-tapped)
Photocells and solar cells: working principles and engineering applications
